DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christine Lopez, T:415.722.2168, plaintiff/moving party, appearing on her own behalf;
Jennifer E. Elgie Paez, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois, T:415.438.6610, for defendants/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was discussed. Plaintiff's request for additional time to file the amended complaint is granted. After hearing, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS ESPECIALLY CATS VETERINARY HOSPITAL, BUTTAR, INC., RUPINDER BUTTAR, MANPRIT BUTTAR, AND GRACE CARTER'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT, modified, as follows:

Defendants Especially Cats Veterinary Hospital, Manprit Buttar, Grace Carter, Buttar, Inc. and Rupinder Buttar's demurrer to the fraud cause of action is SUSTAINED with 30 days leave to amend. Paragraph 47 of the second amended complaint refers to defendants and staff making misrepresentations. Plaintiff should clarify whether it was only the receptionists making the misstatements or whether Dr. Buttar and Ms. Carter also did so. Plaintiff also appears to be alleging that defendants acted fraudulently with regard to Shadow's medical condition. There is no allegation that defendants knew that Shadow had cancer and hid that fact from plaintiff, however. There are insufficient allegations regarding defendants' fraudulent intent or scienter as to the medical condition theory of fraud liability.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christine Lopez, T:415.722.2168, plaintiff/moving party, appearing on her own behalf;
Jennifer E. Elgie Paez, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois, T:415.438.6610, for defendants/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was discussed. Plaintiff's request for additional time to file the amended complaint is granted. After hearing, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, with modification, as follows:

DEFENDANTS ESPECIALLY CATS VETERINARY HOSPITAL, BUTTAR, INC., RUPINDER BUTTAR, MANPRIT BUTTAR, AND GRACE CARTER'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED with 30 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead the fraud cause of action and facts showing a basis to recover punitive damages. The motion is GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to paragraph 42 as there is no basis to recover general/emotional distress damages on the malpractice claim. "Regardless of how foreseeable a pet owner's emotional distress may be in losing a beloved animal, we discern no basis in policy or reason to impose a duty on a veterinarian to avoid causing emotional distress to the owner of the animal being treated, while not imposing such a duty on a doctor to the parents of a child receiving treatment." (McMahon v. Craig (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 1502, 1514.) The motion is GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to paragraph 71 because a party can recover emotional distress damages on a fraud claim. "That general damages for mental pain and suffering are recoverable in a tort action of deceit is established by the cases." (Sprague v. Frank J. Sanders Lincoln Mercury, Inc. (1981) 120 Cal.App.3d 412, 417.) The motion to strike is GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to Rupinder Buttar and she is dismissed from the case. Plaintiff admits that she only named Ms. Buttar because she is a co-owner or corporate officer of Buttar, Inc. The general rule is that shareholders and officers are not liable for corporate debts. Without further allegations, there is no basis to name Ms. Buttar.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.
